action.reasoning: Observation: DKT consistently peaks at ~0.760-0.761 val_auc. The model overfits after ~27 epochs regardless of regularization. Analysis: I need a fundamentally different approach. The original DKT paper used one-hot input vectors of size 2*num_skills. Let me try: 1) GRU with one-hot input (original DKT), 2) Try a different architecture. Also, I should try an ensemble of multiple models. Let me implement the original DKT with one-hot input and also try a model with more capacity but stronger regularization. The key insight is that learned embeddings might be overfitting - one-hot input forces the model to learn from scratch without embedding overfitting. Also, let me try using variational dropout (same dropout mask across time steps) which is known to help LSTMs generalize better.
action.command: MODEL_TYPE=dkvmn EMBED_DIM=50 HIDDEN_DIM=200 DROPOUT=0.2 LR=1e-3 EPOCHS=200 PATIENCE=30 WEIGHT_DECAY=1e-5 python train.py
